finding: review-dispatcher uses Sonnet to select agents based on diff content; file-pattern rules in dispatch-rules.json already handle the mandatory dispatch and could be extended to cover edge-case-hunter and simplicity-enforcer.
detail: review-dispatcher (Sonnet) is invoked on every PR. dispatch-rules.json already routes mandatory agents deterministically. Optional-agent decisions ('logic branches, loops, error paths' => edge-case-hunter; 'abstractions' => simplicity-enforcer) are pattern-matchable on the diff and do not need inference.
recommendation: Extend dispatch-rules.json with regex triggers for edge-case-hunter (control-flow tokens in Python diffs) and simplicity-enforcer (new class/function definitions). Only escalate to the LLM dispatcher when no rule fires.

finding: phase-status assesses phase health (ON TRACK / AT RISK / BLOCKED) via LLM reasoning when the rules are deterministic thresholds.
detail: Rules are explicit: AT RISK if any item blocked >2 sessions; BLOCKED if active item stuck >3 attempts. Both are arithmetic over .work/session.json.
recommendation: Add a small phase_health.py that reads .work/session.json and returns {status, reason, evidence}. Call it from phase-status.

finding: agent-evaluator's determinism check is itself an LLM call; deterministic-eligible proposal classes could be pre-rejected without inference.
detail: Known-deterministic categories (regex matching, schema validation, counting, status lookup, known-category classification) should fail a screener before reaching the agent-evaluator prompt.
recommendation: Pre-screen proposals against a hard-coded list; auto-REJECT with reason if a match fires. Only ambiguous proposals reach the LLM evaluator.

finding: requirements flow synthesises docs/spec/prd.md from operator answers via LLM where placeholder substitution would suffice.
detail: Five structured answers are mapped into known template sections; this is string interpolation, not reasoning.
recommendation: Convert prd-template.md to use named placeholders; add a fill_prd_template.py.

finding: evidence-checker prompt verifies artefact existence (files, ADRs, CI results) using LLM where file/grep checks are sufficient.
detail: All checks listed reduce to os.path.exists / grep / section-present checks per acceptance criterion.
recommendation: Implement check_acceptance_criteria.py and surface it via am_i_done.py --check acceptance-evidence; retire the LLM agent for this path.

finding: new-decision flow uses LLM to produce ADR filename slug from operator-provided title.
detail: Lowercase + non-alnum-to-hyphen + truncate. Pure text transform.
recommendation: Add a make_adr_slug() helper used by the new-decision flow.
